title: Recall and precision in retrieval
description: What recall@k and precision@k measure and why first-stage retrieval optimizes recall while re-ranking optimizes precision.
body: Use when reasoning about which retrieval metric to optimize at each stage. Recall@k is the share of relevant documents found in the top-k; precision@k is the share of the top-k that are relevant. Do not optimize precision in first-stage retrieval — if a relevant document is not retrieved, no reranker can recover it. Actions: tune first-stage retrieval for recall with a generous k, then tune re-ranking for precision on that shortlist. Validation signals include a recall floor at the first stage and a precision gain after re-ranking. Risk boundary: a high-precision but low-recall first stage permanently loses answers; if recall is the bottleneck, escalate the retrieval budget rather than tightening the reranker.
